revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''中和田横穴墓群'''（なかわだおうけつぼぐん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]中和田にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]](横穴式の集...'
revision.text:
'''中和田横穴墓群'''（なかわだおうけつぼぐん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]中和田にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]](横穴式の集合[[墓|墳墓]])である
==概要== 中和田地区の南傾斜面に分布している。多摩地域最大規模の横穴墓群である。
1976年に14基で調査が実施された。14基は2群、5グループに分かれ、玄室平面が単室長方形から胴張複室、さらに単室長方形への変遷が推定されている。7世紀から8世紀の築造とみられる。
== 参考文献 ==
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧#東京都]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかわたおうけつほくん}} [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:東京都の歴史]] [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:多摩市]]

'''中和田横穴墓群'''（なかわだおうけつぼぐん）は、[[東京都]][[多摩市]]中和田にある[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]](横穴式の集合[[墓|墳墓]])である
==概要== 中和田地区の南傾斜面に分布している。多摩地域最大規模の横穴墓群である。
1976年に14基で調査が実施された。14基は2群、5グループに分かれ、玄室平面が単室長方形から胴張複室、さらに単室長方形への変遷が推定されている。7世紀から8世紀の築造とみられる。
== 参考文献 == * 大塚初重・小林三郎・熊野正也 「日本古墳大辞典」p429 東京堂出版 1989年
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧#東京都]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかわたおうけつほくん}} [[Category:群集墳]] [[Category:東京都の歴史]] [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:多摩市]]

[[1976年]](昭和51年)に14基で調査が実施された。14基は2群、5グループに分かれ、玄室平面が単室長方形から胴張複室、さらに単室長方形への変遷が推定されている。[[7世紀]]から[[8世紀]]の築造とみられる。
== 参考文献 == * 大塚初重・小林三郎・熊野正也 『日本古墳大辞典』p429、東京堂出版、1989年
==関連事項== *[[日本の古墳一覧#東京都]]

== 参考文献 == * 大塚初重・小林三郎・熊野正也 『日本古墳大辞典』p429、東京堂出版、1989年

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | dimensions = | burial_goods = | designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|Location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Tumulus Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]]

It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Tumulus Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]]

It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Tumulus Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:Kk]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[ja:中和田横穴墓群]]

It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Tumulus Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == Related Topics == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Tokyo]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Tama City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Link complete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::bump 1-20]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | dimensions = | burial_goods = | designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | dimensions = | burial_goods = | designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | dimensions = | burial_goods = | designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989.== See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} revision.comment: puttin it at the bottom too ant not just in the comment
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | dimensions = | burial_goods = | designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site Map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | dimensions = | burial_goods = | designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | other_designation = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=draft}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster}}

These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster}}

These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}}

These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] {{citation needed|Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{citation needed|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=draft}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] {{citation needed|Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{citation needed|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster}} {{citation needed|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]]

These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中和田横穴墓群|version=100124309|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:History of Tokyo Prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Nakawada, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中和田横穴墓群|version=100124309|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:多摩市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:History of Tokyo Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}} located in Nakawada, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中和田横穴墓群|version=100124309|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:多摩市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:History of Tokyo Prefecture]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | image = | caption = | cluster = | location = Nakawada, Tama City, Tokyo, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35|38|42.6|N|139|25|40.1|E|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | type = Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | construction = 7th–8th century | burial = | area = | burial_goods = | Historic Site = | notes = | map = Japan Tokyo | map_dot_label = Nakawada
Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} located in Nakawada, [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中和田横穴墓群|version=100124309|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tama City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:多摩市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tokyo Prefecture]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]]

These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中和田横穴墓群|version=100124309|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] [[sn:Nakawada Yokohama Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tama]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tokyo Prefecture]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster | position = bottom | mark = Red pog.svg | marksize = 10 | relief = 1 | width = 250 | caption2 = {{Left|location map.}} }} '''Nakawada Rock-Cut Tomb Cluster''' (中和田横穴墓群) is a {{ill|rock-cut tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock-cut tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横穴墓}} located in Nakawada, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, Japan. It is the largest cluster of its kind in the Tama region. == Overview == The cluster is distributed along the south-facing slopes of the Nakawada area.{{Cite web|author=Tokyo Metropolitan Regional Education Support Division Management Section|url=https://tokyo-iseki.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/|title=Tokyo Archaeological Site map Internet Service|work=Tokyo Metropolitan Board of Education|accessdate=2024-04-24}} In 1976, an excavation of 14 tombs was conducted. These 14 tombs are grouped into two clusters and further divided into five groups. The floor plans of the burial chambers exhibit an evolutionary transition from single rectangular chambers to bilobed chambers and back to single rectangular chambers. These tombs are believed to have been constructed between the 7th and 8th centuries. == notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Otsuka Hatsushige, Kobayashi Saburo, and Kumano Masaya, ''Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia), p.429, Tokyodo Shuppan, 1989. == See Also == * [[List of kofun in Japan#Tokyo]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|中和田横穴墓群|version=100124309|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q17226537|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:History of Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Tokyo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tama]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
